topic: A person finds out their biography was written without their knowledge
raw_output:
[story 1]

Arthur slumped deeper into the worn armchair, the cheap fabric protesting beneath his weight. He stared at the glossy printout spread across his lap, the typeface mocking him with its smooth, professional sheen. Every sentence was a calculated move, every anecdote a carefully polished gem. He reread the section detailing his 'pivotal moments,' a section he’d never authorized. A low, irritated hum vibrated in his chest. He pinched the bridge of his nose, the gesture tight and sharp. Someone had taken his life, his messy, complicated existence, and sculpted it into this neat, sterile narrative. He wanted to rip the paper in half, but his hands felt strangely heavy, sluggish with a simmering resentment. He pushed the document away with unnecessary force, the sound a small, frustrated snap in the quiet room.

[story 2]

Elara paced the length of her small apartment, her footsteps sharp and uneven on the hardwood floor. She stopped abruptly by the window, staring out at the indifferent city lights. The air felt thick, pressing down on her shoulders. She picked up the document, the paper crinkling under her tense grip. The prose spoke of her triumphs, her failures, all filtered through an external, judgmental lens. A tight knot formed beneath her sternum, a solid, unpleasant pressure. She let out a slow, audible exhale, a sound laced with deep dissatisfaction. "Unbelievable," she muttered, the word tasting like ash on her tongue. She dropped the paper onto the cluttered desk, the resulting clatter sounding like a final, definitive dismissal of the document’s existence.

[story 3]

Marcus leaned against the cool metal of the filing cabinet, his arms crossed tightly over his chest. He watched the sunlight slant across the dusty floorboards, feeling a distinct aversion to the brightness. The biography lay open on the table, its carefully constructed narrative an affront to his sense of self. He traced a line of text with a restless finger, his jaw clenching so hard a muscle twitched near his ear. A deep sigh escaped him, heavy and laden with unspoken disapproval. He straightened up slowly, his movements deliberate and stiff, as if moving through thick water. He didn't bother to look at the document again; the sheer audacity of it was enough to settle a profound sense of vexation deep within him.
